mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals resilient consumer demand, which drives upward earnings revisions for discretionary and staple sectors and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of months when RSAFS is rising, or the Rank IC of RSAFS changes drops below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that raises corporate default risk and triggers broad-based risk-off selling, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of an elevated federal funds rate and contracting industrial production creates a toxic combination of tight credit and weakening output that amplifies downside equity risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO achieves a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign consistency of negative returns falls below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, month-over-month industrial production growth signals a rebound in manufacturing activity and inventory restocking, which lifts cyclical corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of instances when INDPRO rises during post-shock recovery periods, or the mean return in these windows is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding a 0.5 percent threshold signals above-trend aggregate demand that reduces recession probability, compresses the equity risk premium, and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher than zero in triggered windows using a one-sided t-test at the 5 percent level, or the positive hit rate falls below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation above 1 percent over three months increases the probability of near-term monetary tightening, raises real discount rates applied to future earnings, and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of triggered instances, or the mean forward return in these windows is not significantly below zero at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects broad-based labor and residential investment strength that boosts corporate revenue visibility, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and HOUST produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of joint-expansion months, or the interaction Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal accelerating residential investment and construction employment, which raises demand for building materials and credit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of months when HOUST increases, or the mean forward return in triggered windows is not significantly above zero at the 5 percent level.
